target: srg(1666,105,0,7) non-existence via the bipartite double DRG {105,104,98,7,1;1,7,98,104,105}; Belousova, Makhnev & Tokbaeva (2026), Vestnik Perm. Univ. 1(72), 29-34, DOI 10.17072/1993-0550-2026-1-29-34 (Russian)
finding: The published proof of Theorem 1 does NOT decide the parameter set: its contradiction is arithmetically vacuous and the triple-intersection method leaves the array feasible. Reported as an audit; NOT a claim that the graph exists or does not exist. Treat srg(1666,105,0,7) as OPEN.

canonical_cross_check_sage_drg.tool: sage-drg (Vidali) in SageMath 10.9 (native arm64), the package the authors cite
canonical_cross_check_sage_drg.verdict: sage-drg does NOT rule out this array (feasible/open)

sage_srg_existence_oracle.meaning: SageMath's built-in strongly-regular-graph existence database classifies (1666,105,0,7) as OPEN (not False/nonexistent)

kernel.backend: Lean 4.31 kernel via Docker (leibniz-lean-repl:v4.31.0), plain decide
